kill_fact: The bull's load-bearing claim — live ~$12.80 sits BELOW a 'resolved' deal value of $14.23, delivering +11% on close in days-to-weeks — is false. Only $8.11 of the consideration is hard cash; the other ~$6.12/sh is 0.2215 shares of an UNLISTED, never-traded NewCo bank holding company. Per Citi's fairness opinion (DEFM14A / Form 425), $14.23-$19.18 is an ILLUSTRATIVE range that credits that stub at 1.00x-1.81x tangible book; NewCo pro-forma TBV is only ~$490M (~$355M / ~$6.12 per GDOT share to former holders). The $14.23 'conservative low end' is NOT conservative — it marks a brand-new small-cap bank (born of a forced restructuring of a payments-disintermediation-exposed prepaid/BaaS issuer, with admitted forced index/mandate selling at the open) at FULL 1.00x book. At a realistic newly-listed-bank discount (~0.7-0.8x TBV) the stub is worth ~$3.7-4.9 and realized value is ~$11.8-$13.0 — i.e. the market's $12.80 IS the correct mark, not an arb discount. There is no genuine spread to capture; the stock is at fair, not below it. Compounding this: the bull's 'close by ~06-30, days-to-weeks' timeline is wrong — the 06-23 vote only clears the SHAREHOLDER condition, while the binding gate (Fed / Utah DFI / ASBD approval to FORM a new BHC) is multi-month; the merger agreement's outside date is Nov 23, 2026 PLUS a 90-day auto-extension. Single-binary stub with no hard floor (on a break the holder owns standalone no-moat equity, not the $8.11 cash), trading-through on a mark-to-model unlisted share. Default REFUTED stands.
corrections: If retained at all, this is NOT a +11% days-to-weeks arb. Reframe: (1) target_px must be re-derived from a defensible NewCo TBV multiple — base case ~0.75x TBV gives ~$11.9-12.6 blended, so live $12.80 is at-or-above fair, NOT below a $14.23 target; the $14.23/$19.18 figures are Citi illustrations, not a floor and not a base target. (2) downside_floor of $11.80 is the unaffected standalone price of a no-moat franchise, not a cash-backed floor — on a deal break the holder owns equity, not $8.11. (3) Timeline: model close as Q3/Q4-2026 (regulatory BHC formation), not 06-30; outside date Nov-23-2026 + 90-day extension. (4) The 06-23 vote is necessary but NOT the value-resolving event — the unlisted-stub mark and the multi-month Fed/Utah/ASBD gate are. conviction_cap held low: asymmetry is fabricated (live ~= fair), the binary lacks a hard floor, and the 'win' depends on an unlisted stub re-rating above book that management itself calls performance-dependent.
evidence: DEFM14A filed 2026-05-08 (SEC, Green Dot Corp CIK 1386278): Citi selected-public-companies analysis implies $14.23-$19.18/sh INCLUDING $8.11 cash, using ~1.00x-1.81x tangible-book multiples applied to the Combined Company's estimated TBV at 6/30/2026 close — i.e. an illustrative range, not a contractual floor; only the $8.11 cash leg is fixed.

Form 425 town-hall (SEC, 2025-11): NewCo pro-forma tangible book ~$490M; former GDOT holders own ~72% (~$355M, ~$6.12/sh at 1.00x TBV); CommerceOne CEO Kenneth Hill states the upper end is performance-dependent ('performance will drive that ... the market will reward you with a higher multiple') — confirming the stub value is a mark-to-model on an unlisted share, not resolved value.

DEFM14A / 8-K (SEC, 2026): merger outside/termination date is November 23, 2026 with an automatic 90-day extension if regulatory approvals are not obtained; closing requires Federal Reserve, Utah DFI and ASBD approval to FORM a new bank holding company — confirming a multi-month regulatory gate, refuting the 'close by ~06-30 / days-to-weeks' timeline (the 06-23 vote clears only the shareholder condition).

MarketBeat quote 2026-06-22 11:12 ET: GDOT $12.80; sell-side consensus Hold, ~$14.75 PT (range incl. a Sell) — the market is pricing $8.11 cash + an unlisted stub at ~0.7-0.8x TBV, i.e. live price is at fair, not at an ~11% discount to a captured deal value.
